mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over recent months signals easing financial conditions and lower discount rates, which lifts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL inflation pressure and the policy rate remains above the price level trend, real rates tighten and equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the spread between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL changes does not improve out-of-sample IC relative to single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE over a short window signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which reduces cyclically sensitive earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day SPX returns are not negative in the majority of cases after a 0.3 pp 3-month rise in UNRATE, or if the signal loses significance once unemployment changes are lagged one month.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail spending, which raises near-term revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if combined positive PAYEMS and RSAFS momentum does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the interaction term is not materially better than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO signals a manufacturing slowdown and weaker aggregate output momentum, which compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX forward returns are not negative after sustained INDPRO declines, or if the rolling 3-year rank IC is below 0.03 after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls sharply while UNRATE rises, the housing market is confirming a demand downturn that leads equities lower over the next 20 trading days through weaker growth expectations and tighter credit conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of falling HOUST and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of triggered cases, or if the interaction adds no incremental explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS count with flat or declining UNRATE signals improving labor absorption and stronger household demand, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if improving PAYEMS combined with flat or lower UNRATE does not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if the effect disappears after adjusting for lagged market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening inflation-control regime, a falling CPIAUCSL alongside stable or rising FEDFUNDS improves real rate expectations and helps equities by anchoring inflation risk; the effect is strongest over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS condition fails to generate positive 5-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if performance is no better than using CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS alone.
